prompt: I have customer churn data with 5000 rows — binary outcome (churned/not), plus age, tenure_months, monthly_spend, and support_tickets_last_90d. Build me a Bayesian logistic regression to predict churn probability with uncertainty estimates.
assertions: Uses Bernoulli likelihood (pm.Bernoulli) with logit inverse-link function
Standardizes or scales predictors before fitting the model
Sets weakly informative priors on regression coefficients (Normal with sigma between 1 and 5)
Calls pm.sample() for MCMC inference without hardcoding chains (no chains=4 or similar)
Uses az.summary() or az.plot_trace() for convergence diagnostics
Reports 94% HDI (not 95% CI) for parameter estimates
Uses coords and dims in the PyMC model definition
Documents prior choices with justification comments in the code
Uses a descriptive random seed (sum(map(ord, ...)) pattern or similar), not a magic number like 42
Runs calibration checks using ArviZ (plot_ppc_pit or similar), not custom plotting code
Saves InferenceData to disk (idata.to_netcdf or similar) after sampling
